title: pedicularis comosa subsp. nevadensis
intro: Pedicularis comosa subsp. nevadensis, also known as Nevada lousewort, is a plant species belonging to the balsamroot family (Orobanchaceae) and is native to the western United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Nevada lousewort can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
